issue_title: Poor label quality: "Subscribe to our mailing list"
actual_result: Label describes the action/purpose rather than what to enter in the field; User may not immediately understand they need to provide their email address; Label conflates the call-to-action with the field requirement
expected_result: Form field labels should be descriptive and meaningful
recommendation: Change label to 'Email address' or 'Your email' to clearly indicate the expected input type, while keeping the subscription context in surrounding text or instructions

issue_title: Required field not clearly indicated: "Subscribe to our mailing list"
actual_result: Required field status is not communicated in the visible label text; No instructions or aria-describedby text to convey required status; Required attribute is set programmatically, but not surfaced to users visually or verbally; Users may not realize this field is mandatory before attempting to submit
expected_result: Required field status should be communicated visually and programmatically
recommendation: Update the label text to include a required indicator, such as 'Subscribe to our mailing list *' or 'Subscribe to our mailing list (required)'. Alternatively, add aria-describedby text that states 'This field is required.' This ensures all users, including those using assistive technologies, understand the field is mandatory before submission.

actual_result: Missing role="img" — SVG is not identified as an image to assistive technologies; No title element — no accessible name provided; No aria-label — no fallback text alternative; No aria-labelledby — no reference to a title ID; SVG has no accessible name and will be skipped by screen readers
expected_result: SVGs should have role="img" and an accessible name via title + aria-labelledby
recommendation: Add role="img" and either: (1) include a <title id="unique-id"> element with descriptive text and set aria-labelledby="unique-id", or (2) add aria-label="descriptive text". Example: <svg role="img" aria-labelledby="svg-title"><title id="svg-title">Descriptive title</title>...</svg>
